pr_title: [DOM-55668] Improvements for Flows-related CC vals

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
ok @ddl-ebrown  think here's where dev-v2 will get the vals.

though im not sure what to do about the `com.cerebro.domino.flows.enabled` one. we don't want to just add a default "enabled=true" here, need to ref the deployment config and not sure how to do that correctly here. at the very least, for now it could just be a "known issue" that on dev-v2 if you want to use flows you have to do `bazel run //nucleus -- --jvm_flag=-Dcom.cerebro.domino.flows.enabled=true`

cc @ddl-jbrown

comments.author: ddl-ebrown
comments.body: Yeah, basically I wanted to make sure when we're NOT doing an install using k8s / helm charts that we still get the right default values during development.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: i'll add this to the dev-v2 and flows runbooks

pr_title: [PLAT-8119] Fix Pod Networking

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body:
Would doing something like this be possible? Just checking if we can set EKS version at one place and keep kubectl version synchronized.

```suggestion
  default = "https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/amazon-eks/${var.eks_version}/2023-08-16/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl"
```

comments.author: ddl-pjohnson
comments.body: I think I just copy-pasta'd this from the existing script, I can't find a download url that only has `major.minor` in it, so we would still need the patch version. I can add logic to just extract `major.minor` from the full version I guess.

pr_title: [PLAT-8119] Fix Pod Networking

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body: Is there a dep that needs Go 1.19? If I recall, we've been installing 1.17 since `dino` hasn't been updated to a newer Go version.

comments.author: ddl-pjohnson
comments.body: 1.17 isn't available in linuxbrew anymore, so had to update unfortunately. Ideally we will move away from installing language runtimes using brew.

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body: Ah, ok. Thank you for the update.

pr_title: [PLAT-8119] Fix Pod Networking

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body: Does Homebrew need the `packaging` library? I'm somewhat hesitant on passing `--break-system-packages` when building the AMI.

comments.author: ddl-pjohnson
comments.body: Not homebrew specifically, but ansible needs it for running pip install. I think this is probably a bug with brew ansible, I can add a comment.

comments.author: ddl-pjohnson
comments.body: Actually, just removed this since it's not necessary anymore.

pr_title: [PLAT-8119] Fix Pod Networking

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body:
This may be similar to those Python packages we used to install on Mac and are not really used. Have you tried removing this block?

I'm guessing that we'd only need to pass `--break-system-packages` for "developer preferences": https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/develop/dev/setup/ansible/roles/common/tasks/user_preferences.yml#L42-L56. That playbook is still referencing Python 3.10. It's probably not used by anyone at the moment.

comments.author: ddl-pjohnson
comments.body: I've been a bit reluctant to remove this, since it is part of the "interface" that dev-v2 is offering e.g. are there scripts that depend on pyyaml implicitly? On the other hand it's time we stopped doing that and made people set up proper virtual envs, so I'll yeet this like we did locally.

pr_title: [PLAT-8119] Fix Pod Networking

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body: Are these messages written to a log file for every node startup and shutdown, or are they mainly for reference if someone ran the script manually?

comments.author: ddl-pjohnson
comments.body: The script is run by systemd, which has it's own logging mechanism. I was mostly using it as a reference when testing, since it's hard to see what is happening when a node is restarting. I can remove if you want?

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body: No biggie, we can keep it. I was mainly curious if we'd have to check some log file in case the script doesn't run.

comments.author: fernandoacorreia
comments.body:
@dmcwhorter-ddl I don't understand why the model name and version are optional if the source isn't. What's the situation when we want to have a source with the model name or version, or both, missing?

```
final case class ModelDeploymentModelDetailSource (
  registeredModelName: Option[String],
  registeredModelVersion: Option[Int],
  registeredModelType: Option[String]
)
```

comments.author: ddl-yaniv-amar
comments.body: Thanks @fernandoacorreia . I agree, it should not be optional. I'll discuss with David and get it updated. Assigning the ticket to myself.

pr_title: [DOM-54528] Create and Update APIs for Compute Providers Admin API

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body:
I'm not sure if exceptions are the right thing to be throwing for validation errors, but I'm also not sure if there's a better way to aggregate errors for all fields.
Is this the right approach for scala or should I be doing some kind of Either chaining?

comments.author: dmcwhorter-ddl
comments.body:
I think it would be ideal to 
1. return all errors for all fields
2. not use exceptions but rather normal control logic to accumulate validation failures
However I think we could capture this in a nice to have ticket for later and this is fine as a starting point
